name: Context Menu Handlers — Explorer Shell Extensions
categoryDescription: Registry keys that cause code to execute at boot, logon, or via Windows subsystem hooks.
description: COM objects registered as context menu handlers — loaded into Explorer when the user right-clicks any file.
forensic_value: Context menu handlers execute within Explorer whenever any file is right-clicked — trivial to trigger and hard to notice. Any unexpected CLSID here pointing to a non-standard DLL is suspicious.
